description: Star allele definitions for pharmacogenetic variants in the main schema. Only pharmacogenetic variants with haplotype complexity are included here.

*1.clinical_interpretation: Normal VKORC1 expression. Standard warfarin dosing appropriate.

warfarin_dosing.recommendation: Use significantly lower initial dose; increase INR monitoring
warfarin_dosing.warning: High bleeding risk. INR should be monitored more frequently initially.

high_sensitivity.cpic_recommendation: Significantly reduce dose; more frequent INR monitoring

important_notes: VKORC1 genotyping is FDA-approved for warfarin dosing
Dosing should also consider CYP2C9 and CYP4F2 variants (epistasis)
The IWPC algorithm combines VKORC1 + CYP2C9 + CYP4F2 + clinical factors for dose estimation
rs9923231 allele orientation may vary by platform; verify against CPIC/PharmGKB

*3.definition: Loss of function (Ile359Leu - rs1057910 T allele)

intermediate_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: Reduce initial dose; monitor drug levels where available

poor_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: Significantly reduce dose; frequent monitoring essential

important_notes: CYP2C9 genotyping is FDA-approved for warfarin dosing
In warfarin calculation, CYP2C9 effect is additive with VKORC1 and CYP4F2
For NSAIDs: CYP2C9 poor metabolizers have increased GI bleeding risk
For phenytoin: poor metabolizers need dose reduction to avoid toxicity
Your schema includes rs1057910 (CYP2C9*3) but MISSING rs1799853 (CYP2C9*2) - recommend adding

warfarin_dosing.recommendation: No dose adjustment based on CYP4F2 alone
May require slightly higher warfarin dose (vitamin K not catabolized as efficiently)

important_notes: CYP4F2 has tertiary importance in warfarin dosing (after VKORC1 and CYP2C9)
Explains only ~7% of warfarin dose variance
Your schema correctly marks as 'secondary' role
Effect is opposite to VKORC1: reduced function = REQUIRES HIGHER dose
Usually only included in IWPC algorithm for precision dosing studies

CYP2D6.note: COMPLEX GENE - Single SNP insufficient for accurate phenotyping

*4.definition: Loss of function (622-647delTAGG deletion - rs3892097 splicesite)

variants.annotation: Splice site variant, nonsense-mediated decay, non-functional protein

*5.definition: Gene deletion (whole gene copy number loss)

ultrarapid_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: May need higher doses or alternative drug
ultrarapid_metabolizer.note: Requires gene duplication/multiplication detection - not covered by single SNP

poor_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: Significantly reduce dose or switch to alternative drug

important_limitations: ❌ CRITICAL: Your schema only includes rs3892097, which only detects *4 allele (frameshift)
❌ MISSING: Gene deletion detection (copy number variation / CNV) - cannot detect *5, *6, *7, etc.
❌ MISSING: Gene duplication detection - cannot detect ultrarapid metabolizers (*1xN)
⚠️ WARNING: Single SNP genotyping is INSUFFICIENT for clinical CYP2D6 interpretation
✓ rs3892097 is valuable for detecting common loss-of-function variant, but requires supplementation
CYP2D6.recommendation: For comprehensive CYP2D6 phenotyping, use either:
options: 3. Next-generation sequencing (gold standard for all alleles)
4. Commercial CYP2D6 diplotyping panel (e.g., GeneSight, Myriad)
CYP2D6.current_schema_usage: Your variant is appropriate for pharmacogenomics awareness, but note phenotype interpretation limitations in clinical reports

*2.clinical_notes: Reduced warfarin metabolism; impaired clopidogrel activation

variants.annotation: i2C>T, splicesite variant, loss of function

poor_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: Avoid drug or use alternative with reduced dose

important_notes: CYP2C19 impairs clopidogrel activation (prodrug requiring metabolism)
Poor metabolizers have reduced antiplatelet effect on clopidogrel
Consider prasugrel or ticagrelor as alternatives for poor metabolizers

cyp3a5_nonexpressor.cpic_recommendation: Use standard to lower tacrolimus doses

poor_metabolizer.cpic_recommendation: Reduce dose by 90% or avoid drug

important_notes: FDA-approved genotyping for thiopurine dosing
Poor metabolizers at severe risk of bone marrow suppression and life-threatening toxicity
FDA black box warning for 5-FU and DPYD deficiency
Poor metabolizers at severe risk of life-threatening toxicity
Genotyping strongly recommended before 5-FU treatment

poor_glucuronidator.cpic_recommendation: Reduce irinotecan dose by 25-50%; increase monitoring

important_notes: CPIC guideline for irinotecan dosing based on UGT1A1 genotype
rs3064744 is a repeat expansion (TATA box region) - may require special genotyping
Gilbert syndrome (TA7/TA7) affects bilirubin metabolism

Kaiping, Anant, Dhon, Sapporo-like, Wose.definition: Kaiping, Anant, Dhon, Sapporo-like, Wosera allele

Beverly Hills, Genova, Iwate, Niigata, Y.definition: Beverly Hills, Genova, Iwate, Niigata, Yamaguchi allele

Seattle, Lodi, Modena, Ferrara II, Athen.definition: Seattle, Lodi, Modena, Ferrara II, Athens-like allele

Mediterranean, Dallas, Panama, Sassari,.definition: Mediterranean, Dallas, Panama, Sassari, Cagliari, Birmingham allele

g6pd_deficiency.cpic_recommendation: Avoid oxidative stressors; use alternative drugs

important_notes: Most common enzymatic deficiency worldwide (~400M affected)
Risk of severe hemolytic anemia with oxidative stressors
Important in neonatal jaundice management
High prevalence in East Asian populations (~50%)
Flushing reaction to alcohol due to acetaldehyde accumulation
Increased cardiovascular and cancer risk with alcohol exposure

*2.clinical_notes: T carriers have lower P-gp expression

important_notes: ABCB1 is the major efflux transporter for many drugs
rs1045642 is synonymous but affects mRNA stability and protein expression
3435C>T is associated with altered digoxin absorption

reduced.cpic_recommendation: May require dose adjustment for affected drugs

*1F.clinical_notes: A carriers metabolize caffeine more slowly

validation_summary.recommendation: Add rs1799853 to CYP2C9 section for complete warfarin pharmacogenetics

usage_example.scenario: Patient genotyped with rs9923231 (T/T), rs1057910 (T/C), rs2108622 (C/T)

interpretation.VKORC1: T/T = homozygous alternate = CYP2C9 *3/*3 = HIGH SENSITIVITY (points: 2)
interpretation.warfarin_recommendation: SIGNIFICANTLY REDUCE INITIAL DOSE (50% reduction recommended)
interpretation.clinical_action: Start at 2.5-3mg/day, check INR after 3-5 days, adjust per IWPC algorithm
